en: But you don't actually have to keep doing that.
ko: 하지만 계속 그렇게 할 필요는 없어.

en: Oh, force of habit. Here you go. Swiped these from the tech fridge.
ko: 아, 습관이야. 여기, 기술 냉장고에서 가져왔어.

en: Oh, nice going. Want a slice?
I noticed a couple hours ago you had a meeting with another possible CEO.
ko: 몇 시간 전에 다른 CEO 후보와 회의한 걸 봤어.

en: I saw him arrive. How'd that go?
ko: 그가 도착하는 걸 봤어. 어떻게 됐어?

en: Until he called us to leave. The term he used was a chick site.
ko: 우리가 나가라고 할 때까지. 그가 쓴 용어는 '여성 사이트'였어.

en: And I didn't hear anything he said after that.
ko: 그 이후로는 그의 말을 듣지 못했어.

en: Apparently, selling clothes makes us a chick site. They weren't really? How is this all legit?
ko: 아무래도 옷을 파는 게 우리를 여성 사이트로 만든대. 정말 그럴 수 있어? 이게 다 정당한 건가?

en: I couldn't agree more. I find that surprising.
So, what'd you do for work, then, before you retired?
I was a VP for Dex One. Phone books?
No. I was in charge of printing. Before that, I ran sales.
ko: 아니. 인쇄를 담당했어. 그 전에 영업을 했어.
